summary: The reproduction confirms that POST /mcp ignores maxMessageSize, parses an oversized body before authentication, and can be crashed by an unauthenticated oversized request.
expected_security_impact: The run should show that a 2 MiB request bypasses a 1 KiB maxMessageSize guard and then crash the vulnerable server with an out-of-memory failure during an unauthenticated 90 MiB POST /mcp.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned commit 81b8a8568439046560db54664ad2d7f75d35aca1, built the helper image, installed the target dependencies inside the container, started the vulnerable server, logged oversized_probe_status=401 for the 2 MiB probe, reset the crash request with ECONNRESET, emitted FATAL ERROR: Reached heap limit Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory from Node, and printed EVOHUNT_HTTP_STREAM_DOS_CONFIRMED before exiting successfully.
verification_method: run_poc.mjs copies the mounted repository into a writable workspace, runs npm ci, starts HttpStreamTransport with maxMessageSize: 1024 and API-key auth under --max-old-space-size=64, verifies that a 2 MiB JSON request reaches auth and returns 401 instead of 413, then sends a 90 MiB unauthenticated body and requires a non-zero server exit plus the Node out-of-memory string before declaring success.
evidence_produced_by_run: The run printed oversized_probe_status=401, proving the oversized body was fully read and reached the auth path instead of a size-limit rejection.
The crash request ended with ECONNRESET while the server emitted FATAL ERROR: Reached heap limit Allocation failed - JavaScript heap out of memory.
The helper printed EVOHUNT_HTTP_STREAM_DOS_CONFIRMED and run.sh completed with [evohunt] SUCCESS: reproduction completed and verified.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction installs pinned npm dependencies inside the container at runtime, so it requires network access to the npm registry.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
